epigraph.text: The average man does not want to be free. He wants to be safe.

follyOfTheDay.headline: University Bans Letter 'E' To Combat 'Linguistic Hierarchy'
follyOfTheDay.summary:
A mid-sized California university, in what can only be described as an act of determined self-sabotage, has announced a sweeping initiative to remove the letter 'E'—the most common letter in the English language—from all campus wayfinding and signage. The administration claims this move represents a bold stroke against "linguistic hierarchy" and promotes "inclusive epistemology." The policy went into effect Monday morning, resulting in campus maps directing students to the "LIBARY," the "CAFTARIA," and the "STUDANT CNTR." One building's sign now reads simply "??" where "RESIDENCE" once appeared.

Students and faculty report widespread confusion, with some unable to locate classrooms and others arriving at entirely wrong buildings. A physics professor noted the irony that the initiative makes communication measurably worse for everyone with dyslexia or ESL backgrounds—those the policy purportedly aims to serve. The administration has defended the decision as "progressively symbolic" and warned that "questions about implementation represent epistemic violence." A spokesperson suggested that if students cannot navigate the campus, perhaps that builds "character through adversity." Classes have been consolidated into buildings whose names remain pronounceable.
follyOfTheDay.commentary: In pursuing equity, the university has achieved something far more equal: equal confusion for all.
